---
title: "Introduction to modelling infectious disease and health economics in R"
author: by Lander Willem and colleagues from the University of Antwerp and Hasselt University as part of the SIMID consortium
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false  
---

```{r libs, cache = FALSE, echo = FALSE, results = "hide", warning = FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library('curl'))
options(width = 80)
```

<hr>
<img src="ext_fig/logo_chermid.png" height="50px"> &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
<img src="ext_fig/logo_dsi.png" height="80px"> &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
<img src="ext_fig/logo_simid_yellow.png" height="80px">
<hr>


## Required software
* [R](http://cran.r-project.org) is used as the main programming language. Please install at least version: R-4.0.0.
* [RStudio](http://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/) (recommended) is a popular graphic user interface (GUI).

## Tutorials: general introduction to R and SIR modelling

1. [Introduction to RStudio](intro_to_RStudio.html)
2. [Introduction to R](intro_to_r.html)
3. [Introduction to infectious disease modelling with R](intro_to_SIR.html)

## Tutorials: Individual-Based Models (IBM)

4. [Introduction to Individual-Based Models (IBMs) using R](intro_to_IBM_coding.html)
5. [IBM with random walks](IBM_random_walk.html)
6. [IBM with location-specific mixing](IBM_location.html)

## Tutorials: Cost-effectiveness Analysis (CEA)

7. [CEA: influenza](CEA_flu_basic.html)
8. [CEA: influenza with uncertainty](CEA_flu_uncertainty.html)
9. [CEA: RSV](CEA_RSV_dynamic.html)

Extra:

- [Discounting](example_discounting.html)
- [Parametric sensitity analysis: parameters](PSA_parameters.html)
- [Parametric sensitity analysis: methods](PSA_methods.html)

We provide some background literature [here](background_literature.html).

---

<font size="1"> The R and SIR tutorials are based on work from Sebastian Funk, Gwen Knight and Anton Camacho from the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. We fully acknowledge their work and are thankful for making them open-source via [GitHub](https://github.com/sbfnk/modelling).</font>
